title: Digger Sasaki Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/455000 .

Oral history interview with Digger Sasaki, who was a child at the time of the Japanese American incarceration during WWII. Mr. Sasaki was living in Auburn, Washington and was sent to the Tule Lake concentration camp in California with his family. The Sasakis relocated to Berekley after the war. More than 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
